title: Information Theory for ML
summary: Entropy, mutual information, and KL divergence — the theoretical foundation of loss functions and feature selection.
key_concepts: Entropy H(X) = -Σ p(x) log p(x) — measures uncertainty
Cross-entropy H(P,Q) = -Σ p(x) log q(x) — used as loss function
Mutual Information I(X;Y) — feature relevance measure
Information gain — used in decision trees
ml_connection: Cross-entropy loss for classification. Information gain for tree splits. KL divergence in VAEs and distillation. Mutual information in representation learning.
exercises: Compute entropy of a fair coin vs a biased coin (p=0.9).
Show that cross-entropy loss = negative log likelihood for categorical distribution.
Implement information gain from scratch and use it to pick the best split for a decision tree.
What is the relationship between cross-entropy and KL divergence?
thinking_questions: Why is entropy maximized for a uniform distribution?
Why can KL(P||Q) be infinite even when KL(Q||P) is finite?
